name: Dopaminergic Reward Prediction Error

source.author: Wolfram Schultz, Peter Dayan, P. Read Montague
source.paper: A neural substrate of prediction and reward

biological_basis: Midbrain dopamine neurons fire in proportion to reward prediction error — the difference between received and expected reward. They encode a teaching signal for reinforcement learning.
core_claim: Reward is not absolute but relative to expectation. Unexpected rewards produce positive RPE; omitted expected rewards produce negative RPE. This drives learning of value predictions.

implementable_mechanism.description: Track expected reward (value). On each step, RPE = actual_reward - expected_reward. Update value estimate toward actual. Use RPE as learning signal and intrinsic motivation metric.
implementable_mechanism.pseudocode: rpe = reward - value.predict(state); value.update(state, rpe); return rpe

notes: Maps to RewardSignal tracking prediction error reduction as intrinsic reward.

name: Temporal Difference Learning

source.author: Richard S. Sutton, Andrew G. Barto
source.paper: Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction (2nd ed.), Chapter 6

biological_basis: TD learning combines bootstrapping from current value estimates with temporal credit assignment. The TD error δ = r + γV(s') - V(s) parallels dopaminergic RPE signals.
core_claim: Learn value functions online without waiting for final outcomes. TD error provides a biologically plausible learning signal for sequential decision-making.

implementable_mechanism.description: Maintain value estimate V(s). At each step compute TD error: δ = reward + γ*V(next_state) - V(current_state). Update V toward reducing δ. Intrinsic variant: reward = surprise reduction.
implementable_mechanism.pseudocode: delta = reward + gamma * V(next_state) - V(current_state); V(current_state) += alpha * delta; return delta

notes: EIDOS uses simplified intrinsic TD: reward = previous_prediction_error - current_prediction_error.
